breakpoint_locations.problem: Crosswalk not consulted as fallback. Falls back to raw anchor_name from pz_proj. Should call _resolve_domain_display_label(_z_anchor_id, _z_anchor_name) instead of _z_anchor_name.
Crosswalk not consulted as fallback. Same pattern as SITE-1.
Crosswalk not consulted as fallback. Same pattern as SITE-1.

correctly_implemented_site.status: CORRECT — crosswalk IS consulted via _resolve_domain_display_label() fallback

secondary_blocker.description: semantic_topology_model.json and semantic_topology_layout.json do not exist in repository. Without these, _build_semantic_report_context() returns fallback_available=False and count cards render structural counts (13 Domains) instead of semantic counts (17 Semantic Domains / 5 Backed / 12 Semantic-Only). Crosswalk cannot substitute for topology model in this path.

explanation: The crosswalk is correctly parsed, loaded, and stored in _SEMANTIC_CROSSWALK (a module-level global). It is accessible to all render functions. The function _resolve_domain_display_label() correctly uses the crosswalk as a fallback when the topology model is absent. However, the three primary zone-label render sites (tier1_narrative:3899, tier1_evidence:3204, decision_surface:6744-6746) use _resolve_dom_to_semantic_context() which only checks _SEMANTIC_TOPOLOGY_MODEL. When that function returns empty fields, they fall back directly to the raw anchor_name from the pressure_zone_projection — never calling _resolve_domain_display_label(). The crosswalk is propagated but not reached at these sites. The fix (not implemented here) is to replace the raw anchor_name fallback with a call to _resolve_domain_display_label() at each of the three breakpoint sites.
